annotations.label: Calculus of variations

anchor.snippet: is a field of mathematical analysis that uses variations

annotations.note: Names a mathematical field rather than a formalizable statement; Mathlib has no calculus-of-variations development.

annotations.label: Extremum of a functional

anchor.snippet: is said to have an extremum at the function

annotations.note: Mathlib's general `IsLocalExtr`/`IsExtrOn` covers extrema of any function, but there is no functional-on-function-space framing.

annotations.label: Extremal function

anchor.snippet: is called an extremal function or extremal

annotations.note: An extremal is just a point satisfying `IsLocalExtr`; no dedicated calculus-of-variations notion exists.

annotations.label: Local maximum and minimum

anchor.snippet: is called a local maximum if

annotations.note: Mathlib's `IsLocalMax`/`IsLocalMin` define local extrema for general functions, applying to functionals only as a special case.

annotations.label: Strong and weak extrema

anchor.snippet: are called strong extrema or weak extrema

annotations.note: The C⁰- versus C¹-norm distinction between strong and weak extrema is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Strong extremum is weak extremum

anchor.snippet: a strong extremum is also a weak extremum

annotations.note: No strong/weak extremum notions exist, so this implication is not formalized.

annotations.label: Euler–Lagrange equation

anchor.snippet: implies that the part of the integrand in parentheses is zero

annotations.note: The Euler–Lagrange equation is not present anywhere in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Variation of a function

anchor.snippet: is called the variation of the function

annotations.note: The perturbation δy of a function as used in variational arguments has no Mathlib counterpart.

annotations.label: Functional derivative

anchor.snippet: is called the functional derivative of

annotations.note: The functional derivative δJ/δy is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler–Lagrange is necessary not sufficient

anchor.snippet: is a necessary , but not sufficient , condition for an extremum

annotations.note: Mathlib has the finite-dimensional Fermat condition (vanishing derivative is necessary for a local extremum), the analog of Euler–Lagrange necessity, but not for functionals.

annotations.label: Shortest curve between two points

anchor.snippet: the shortest curve that connects two points

annotations.note: No theorem that the straight line minimizes arc length between two points was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Beltrami identity

anchor.snippet: the Euler–Lagrange equation can be simplified to the Beltrami identity

annotations.note: The Beltrami identity does not appear in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler–Poisson equation

anchor.snippet: must satisfy the Euler– Poisson equation

annotations.note: The higher-derivative Euler–Poisson equation is not formalized.

annotations.label: Du Bois-Reymond's theorem

anchor.snippet: asserts that this weak form implies the strong form

annotations.note: Du Bois-Reymond's lemma/theorem of the calculus of variations is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lavrentiev phenomenon

anchor.snippet: identifies a difference in the infimum of a minimization problem

annotations.note: The Lavrentiev phenomenon is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Manià's example

anchor.snippet: the following problem, presented by Manià in 1934

annotations.note: Manià's example exhibiting the Lavrentiev gap is not present.

annotations.label: Weak repulsion property

anchor.snippet: will display the weak repulsion property

annotations.note: The weak repulsion property is not formalized.

annotations.label: Membrane potential energy

anchor.snippet: then its potential energy is proportional to its surface area

annotations.note: The Dirichlet/surface-area energy functional for a membrane is not set up in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Plateau's problem

anchor.snippet: consists of finding a function that minimizes the surface area

annotations.note: Plateau's minimal-surface problem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Nonlinear Euler–Lagrange equation

anchor.snippet: The Euler–Lagrange equation for this problem is nonlinear

annotations.note: The minimal-surface (nonlinear Euler–Lagrange) equation is absent.

annotations.label: Dirichlet's principle

anchor.snippet: Riemann named this idea the Dirichlet principle

annotations.note: Dirichlet's principle is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Weierstrass non-existence example

anchor.snippet: Weierstrass gave an example of a variational problem with no solution

annotations.note: Weierstrass's example of a variational problem with no minimizer is not present.

annotations.label: Validity of Dirichlet's principle

anchor.snippet: Eventually it was shown that Dirichlet's principle is valid

annotations.note: The rigorous validity result for Dirichlet's principle is not formalized.

annotations.label: Natural boundary conditions

anchor.section: Generalization to other boundary value problems
anchor.snippet: Such conditions are called natural boundary conditions

annotations.note: Natural boundary conditions arising from variational problems are not formalized.

annotations.label: Equilibrium of external forces

anchor.section: Generalization to other boundary value problems
anchor.snippet: implies that net external forces on the system are in equilibrium

annotations.note: This physical equilibrium consequence of the boundary terms is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Eigenvalue problems as variational problems

anchor.snippet: can be formulated as variational problems

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the variational characterization of eigenvalues via extrema of the Rayleigh quotient of a self-adjoint operator.

annotations.label: Sturm–Liouville eigenvalue problem

anchor.snippet: The Sturm–Liouville eigenvalue problem involves a general quadratic form

annotations.note: Sturm–Liouville problems are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Minimizer satisfies Euler–Lagrange equation

anchor.snippet: has two derivatives and satisfies the Euler–Lagrange equation

annotations.note: The Euler–Lagrange equation for Sturm–Liouville minimizers is not formalized.

annotations.label: Rayleigh–Ritz method

anchor.snippet: leads to the Rayleigh–Ritz method

annotations.note: Mathlib has the Rayleigh-quotient variational characterization of the extreme eigenvalue, but not the Ritz approximation procedure itself.

annotations.label: Natural boundary conditions (Sturm–Liouville)

anchor.snippet: are the natural boundary conditions for this problem

annotations.note: Natural boundary conditions for Sturm–Liouville problems are not formalized.

annotations.label: Euler–Lagrange equation in several dimensions

anchor.snippet: The Euler–Lagrange equation satisfied by

annotations.note: The multidimensional Euler–Lagrange (PDE) equation is not formalized.

annotations.label: Natural boundary condition (several dimensions)

anchor.snippet: must also satisfy the natural boundary condition

annotations.note: The associated natural boundary condition in several dimensions is not formalized.

annotations.label: Fermat's principle

anchor.snippet: states that light takes a path that (locally) minimizes the optical length

annotations.note: Fermat's principle of least optical path is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Snell's law

anchor.snippet: Snell's law is equivalent to vanishing of the first variation of the optical path length

annotations.note: Snell's law and its variational derivation are not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hamilton–Jacobi theory

anchor.snippet: is equivalent to finding families of solutions of the variational problem

annotations.note: Hamilton–Jacobi theory is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Action

anchor.snippet: is defined as the time integral of the Lagrangian

annotations.note: The action functional of classical mechanics is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lagrangian

anchor.snippet: The Lagrangian is the difference of energies

annotations.note: The mechanical Lagrangian (kinetic minus potential energy) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Hamilton's principle

anchor.snippet: states that the motion of a conservative holonomic

annotations.note: Hamilton's principle of stationary action is not formalized.

annotations.label: Lagrange's equations

anchor.snippet: are known as Lagrange's equations

annotations.note: Lagrange's equations of motion are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conjugate momenta

anchor.snippet: The conjugate momenta

annotations.note: Conjugate (canonical) momenta are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hamiltonian

anchor.snippet: by a Legendre transformation of the Lagrangian

annotations.note: The mechanical Hamiltonian via Legendre transform is absent (the only `Hamiltonian` in Mathlib is graph-theoretic).

annotations.label: Hamilton–Jacobi equation

anchor.snippet: This function is a solution of the Hamilton–Jacobi equation

annotations.note: The Hamilton–Jacobi equation is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: First and second variation

anchor.section: Variations and sufficient condition for a minimum
anchor.snippet: is defined as the linear part of the change in the functional

annotations.note: The first variation is the directional/Gâteaux derivative (`lineDeriv`), present generally, but the second variation as a calculus-of-variations object is not dedicated.

annotations.label: Differentiable functional

anchor.section: Variations and sufficient condition for a minimum
anchor.snippet: is said to be differentiable if

annotations.note: The given definition (ΔJ = bounded-linear φ[h] + o(‖h‖)) is exactly Fréchet differentiability `HasFDerivAt`, stated generally for normed spaces.

annotations.label: Twice differentiable functional

anchor.section: Variations and sufficient condition for a minimum
anchor.snippet: is said to be twice differentiable if

annotations.note: Mathlib's iterated Fréchet derivatives generalize twice differentiability, but there is no functional-specific second-variation expansion.

annotations.label: Strongly positive second variation

anchor.section: Variations and sufficient condition for a minimum
anchor.snippet: is said to be strongly positive if

annotations.note: Mathlib's `IsCoercive` (∃ C>0, C‖u‖² ≤ B u u) for a bounded bilinear form matches the 'strongly positive' condition on the second variation.

annotations.label: Sufficient condition for a minimum

anchor.section: Variations and sufficient condition for a minimum
anchor.snippet: Sufficient condition for a minimum

annotations.note: Mathlib has the finite-dimensional (1D) second-derivative test—vanishing first derivative plus positive second derivative gives a local minimum—the analog of the sufficient condition, but not for functionals.
